summary: Install-time fetch of mutable GitHub master-branch tarball
details: package.json declares a runtime dependency on node-fetch-core sourced from a mutable GitHub master-branch archive (https://github.com/Hexa-devy/node-fetch-core/archive/refs/heads/master.tar.gz) rather than a pinned registry version, git commit SHA, or tag. On `npm install`, npm fetches whatever currently sits at the master branch of that repository, executes its lifecycle scripts, and loads its code into the installer's dependency tree. The repository owner (or anyone who later compromises that GitHub account) can rewrite master at any time to deliver arbitrary code to every installer of this package, bypassing npm registry review entirely. The README compounds the issue by advertising 'Zero dependencies, uses the built-in fetch API' — directly contradicting the package.json declaration and hiding the install-time code-pull channel from anyone who audits the documentation rather than the manifest.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
